title: Rental Deposit: How Many Months 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Rental Deposit Calculator: How Many Months?
description: Calculate your rental deposit in Argentina. 1-3 months of rent required. Understand returns, deductions & legal limits. Free calculator.
intro: Argentina's rental market has specific legal requirements (DNU 70/2023), plus BCRA indexing (ICL), real estate commissions, and monthly expenses like building maintenance. Find out exactly how much security deposit you'll pay and when you get it back. Our calculator uses 2026 rates and is updated regularly.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **1-3 months of rent**. Direct calculation based on your monthly rent. Find your deposit amount and understand the return process at lease end.
useCases: Calculate security deposits for apartment rentals in Argentina
Understand landlord deposit requirements and payment terms
Verify deposit returns and deduction calculations
Financial planning for expats moving to Argentina
explanation:
Argentina's **rental market** is regulated by the Civil and Commercial Code plus DNU 70/2023 (which partially superseded Law 27,551). There is freedom to negotiate terms around currency, lease length, and update mechanisms.

## How to Calculate

This calculator applies the standard formula for security deposits, checking against typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values. Most deposits fall between 1-3 months of rent, depending on the lease terms and landlord requirements.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for reference only. For major decisions (financial, legal, related to housing), consult with a local professional or real estate advisor. Our values are current for 2026 and reviewed regularly for accuracy.

faq.q: What's the maximum security deposit landlords can require?
faq.a: Law 27,551 caps deposits at 1 month's rent. DNU 70/2023 allows free negotiation, so amounts can go higher depending on the agreement.

faq.q: How long before I get my deposit back?
faq.a: Typically within 30 days after you vacate, minus any legitimate deductions for damage or outstanding rent.

faq.q: What deductions can a landlord make from my deposit?
faq.a: Damages beyond normal wear and tear, unpaid rent, or other breaches outlined in the lease agreement.

faq.q: Can the deposit amount increase during the lease?
faq.a: No, the deposit stays fixed. However, it's updated based on the final month's rent amount when the lease ends.

faq.q: What about interest on the deposit while held?
faq.a: If there's a delay in returning your deposit, the landlord must pay interest (penalty interest for late returns).

faq.q: What counts as damage when returning the apartment?
faq.a: Compare the initial move-in inspection against the final inspection. Normal wear and tear is expected; damage beyond that gets deducted.

faq.q: Can I break my lease early?
faq.a: Yes, with proper notice and payment of early termination fees: 1 month's rent if in year 1, 1.5 months if after year 1. Minimum 3 months' notice required.

faq.q: What guarantees do landlords typically ask for?
faq.a: Common options: proof of ownership (for co-signers), surety insurance, income verification (3x rent), or joint liability guarantees.

faq.q: Is there a security deposit cap in Argentina?
faq.a: Under the older law (27,551): 1 month maximum. With DNU 70/2023: free negotiation, so limits depend on landlord agreement.

faq.q: How is the security deposit updated or adjusted?
faq.a: The deposit uses the final month's rent as the reference. Some agreements tie updates to BCRA indices (ICL), though negotiation is possible.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
